name: Aziz Bhatti Park
desc: A public neighbourhood park named after Major Aziz Bhatti, a Nishan-e-Haider recipient, offering green space and family recreation.
credit: Photo: Syed Irfan Ali S via TripAdvisor

name: Baba Island and Bhit Island
desc: Two densely populated fishing islands in Karachi harbour near Kiamari, home to long-established Sindhi and Baloch fishing communities.
credit: Source: www.dawn.com

name: Bagh-e-Jinnah
desc: A historic public garden adjoining Frere Hall in central Karachi, laid out in the colonial era and known for its old trees and open lawns.
credit: Photo: Ghulam Shahbaz via Google Images

name: Bagh Ibn-e-Qasim Park
desc: One of Karachi's largest seafront parks, on the Clifton coast, with landscaped gardens beside the restored Jehangir Kothari Parade.
credit: Source: lh3.googleusercontent.com

name: Cape Monze (Ras Muari)
desc: Karachi's westernmost headland on the Arabian Sea, marked by rugged cliffs, a lighthouse and small fishing settlements.
credit: Wikimedia Commons

name: Chota Anda and Bara Anda
desc: Two small rocky islets off the Karachi coast near Manora, known among local boaters and anglers.
credit: Google Earth (satellite imagery)

name: Clifton Beach, Karachi
desc: The city's best-known public beach along the Arabian Sea, popular for evening outings, camel and horse rides, and seaside stalls.
credit: Author's own photograph

name: Country Club Cricket Ground
desc: A recreational cricket and country-club facility on the eastern outskirts of Karachi.
credit: Source: dynamic-media-cdn.tripadvisor.com

name: Do Darya
desc: A seafront dining destination on the Korangi Creek in Defence, lined with restaurants and popular for its views over the water.
credit: Wikimedia Commons

name: Elixir Zoo
desc: A private zoo and wildlife attraction in the Karachi area featuring exotic animals and family recreation.
credit: Source: www.facebook.com

name: French Beach, Karachi
desc: A cleaner, quieter beach west of the city lined with private huts, popular for weekend getaways, swimming and snorkelling.
credit: Source: Zameen.com

name: Giant Osyter Rocks
desc: Rocky tidal outcrops along the Karachi coast named for the oysters found on them, popular with anglers.
credit: Wikimedia Commons

name: Gutter Baghicha
desc: One of Karachi's older municipal green spaces in the Lyari area, long the focus of conservation and anti-encroachment efforts.
credit: Photo: Saman Ghani Khan via Herald Dawn

name: Hawke's Bay Beach
desc: A wide sandy beach west of Karachi known for sea-turtle nesting and beach huts, a popular weekend destination.
credit: Photo: Shahid Muhammadi via Tripadvisor

name: Hilal Park
desc: A green public space in the Clifton/Saddar area used for recreation and community gatherings.
credit: Source: lh3.googleusercontent.com

name: Hill Park
desc: A landscaped hillside park in the Shah Faisal/PECHS area offering city views and family recreation.
credit: Wikimedia Commons

name: Indus River Delta
desc: The vast wetland and mangrove delta southeast of Karachi where the Indus River meets the Arabian Sea, a major ecological zone.
credit: Wikimedia Commons

name: Jakhi Bander
desc: Referred to by some scholars as Lahri Bandar or Juna Shah Bandar; a disputed site.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: James Burnes Park
desc: Karachi was once famous for its lush green parks.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Jheel Park lake
desc: A lakeside public park in the PECHS area popular for boating and evening strolls.
credit: Source: lh3.googleusercontent.com

name: Karachi Golf Club
desc: One of Karachi's established golf courses, set amid green space near Karsaz and host to national tournaments.
credit: Photo: ABossMTan via TripAdvisor

name: Karachi Zoo Park
desc: Karachi zoo is the second oldest and the biggest in Pakistan.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Kashmir Amusement Park
desc: A neighbourhood amusement park offering rides and family entertainment.
credit: Source: lh3.googleusercontent.com

name: Kashmir Park
desc: A public park in central Karachi with green space and a small amusement area.
credit: Source: lh3.googleusercontent.com

name: Kidney Hill Park
desc: A large restored hilltop park in the Defence area, one of the city's biggest green spaces, offering skyline views.
credit: Source: Shehri

name: Mangrove Biodiversity Park
desc: A conservation and eco-tourism park on Karachi's coast showcasing the mangrove ecosystem via boardwalks and trails.
credit: Source: Official Website

name: Manora Beach
desc: A quiet beach on the Manora peninsula reached by boat from Kiamari, near the historic lighthouse and cantonment.
credit: Photo: NNI via Tribune

name: Mubarak Beach
desc: A fishing-village beach at Mubarak Village on the coastal road toward Cape Monze, a launch point for boat trips to Churna Island.
credit: Photo: Syed Fasih via TripAdvisor

name: Nathiagali Beach
desc: A local beach along the Karachi coastal belt, used for day trips.
credit: Source: Sandee

name: Nisar Shaheed Park
desc: A large family park in the Defence (DHA) area with gardens, jogging tracks and play areas.
credit: Source: DHA Karachi Official Website

name: Nishtar Park
desc: A large open ground in the Numaish/Soldier Bazaar area historically used for major public and political gatherings.
credit: Source: lh3.googleusercontent.com

name: Paradise Point, Pakistan
desc: A rocky promontory on the Karachi coast once famous for a natural sea arch (since collapsed), a scenic seaside spot.
credit: Wikimedia Commons

name: Russian Beach
desc: A secluded beach near the Karachi harbour and Kiamari area.
credit: Source: Facebook

name: Safari Park
desc: A large wildlife and recreation park in Gulshan-e-Iqbal with open enclosures, boating and a small train, popular with families.
credit: Source: dynamic-media-cdn.tripadvisor.com

name: Sands Pit
desc: A sandy beach west of Karachi adjoining Hawke's Bay, known for sea-turtle nesting and beach huts.
credit: Author's own photograph

name: Shamspir Island
desc: A low-lying mangrove and fishing island off the Karachi coast.
credit: Google Earth (satellite imagery)

name: Sukhan Dam Picnic Point
desc: A small reservoir and picnic spot on the Malir/eastern outskirts of Karachi.
credit: Source: Youtube - Yasir Jawed

name: Tushan Beach
desc: A quieter beach along the Karachi coastal road, popular for day trips.
credit: Source: Sandee

name: Aga Khan Secondary Hospital, Kharadar
desc: Inaugurated in 1924, part of the Aga Khan Health Service network.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Arts Council of Pakistan Karachi
desc: A leading cultural institution in Saddar hosting theatre, music, literature festivals and art exhibitions.
credit: Source: lh3.googleusercontent.com

name: Bahria Icon Tower, Clifton
desc: A supertall skyscraper on Clifton's seafront, among the tallest buildings in Pakistan and a landmark of the modern skyline.
credit: Wikimedia Commons

name: Burhani Hospital
desc: Initially established as a dispensary in 1913.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: City Station
desc: Covers a vast region off I. I. Chundrigar Road.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Civil Hospital Karachi
desc: The Dr. Ruth K.M. Pfau Civil Hospital was founded in 1898 during a third pandemic.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Clifton Bridge
desc: Clifton Bridge, although constructed before partition, was a later addition to this bunch.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Cross Habib Bank Plaza
desc: Near the spot where public transport buses turn onto M. A. Jinnah Road.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: DJ Science College
desc: Like a Keatsian ode - majestic, well constructed and soulful.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Dolmen Mall - Clifton
desc: One of Karachi's most popular shopping malls, on the Clifton seafront with retail, dining and sea views.
credit: Wikimedia Commons

name: Drigh Colony Railway Station
desc: A suburban stop on Karachi's railway network serving the Drigh Road/Shah Faisal area.
credit: Wikimedia Commons

name: Eduljee Dinshaw Charitable Dispensary
desc: At the bazaar, threading through vendors is a task in itself.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Esmailji Amiji Nathani Maternity Home
desc: A maternity home serving Karachi's public health needs.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Expo Centre Karachi
desc: A major convention and exhibition complex in Gulshan-e-Iqbal hosting large trade fairs and public events.
credit: Wikimedia Commons

name: Fire Brigade Station
desc: Flashback to the early 20th century when new buildings were being planned.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Governor House, Karachi
desc: The Governor House is the official residence of the Governor of Sindh province, along Aiwan-e-Sadar Road
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Habib Bank Ltd. (HBL) Plaza
desc: A landmark high-rise on I.I. Chundrigar Road, once the tallest building in Pakistan and headquarters of Habib Bank.
credit: Wikimedia Commons

name: Hub Dam
desc: A large reservoir on the Hub River at the Karachi-Balochistan border, a water source and a Ramsar-listed wetland for migratory birds.
credit: Source: i.brecorder.com

name: Jaffer Fuddoo Dispensary
desc: The clock tower is a unique sight, showing accurate Pakistan Standard Time.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Jinnah International Airport
desc: Pakistan's largest international and domestic airport.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Karachi Municipal Corporation
desc: The historic KMC building, a colonial-era civic landmark in Saddar and seat of the city's municipal administration.
credit: Wikimedia Commons

name: Karachi Port
desc: Pakistan's premier deep-water seaport, developed from the 19th century and among the busiest in South Asia.
credit: Wikimedia Commons

name: Karachi Port Trust (KPT) Building
desc: Designed by George Wittet, the consultant architect of Bombay (now Mumbai).
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Kharadar General Hospital
desc: Previously named Cement Hospital since its inception.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Lady Dufferin
desc: Construction work on the hospital commenced in November 1894.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: LuckyOne Mall
desc: One of Pakistan's largest shopping malls, on Rashid Minhas Road, with extensive retail, dining and entertainment.
credit: Wikimedia Commons

name: Malir railway station
desc: A suburban railway station serving the Malir area of Karachi.
credit: Source: lh3.googleusercontent.com

name: NASTP Silicon Valley Pakistan
desc: The National Aerospace Science and Technology Park, a technology and aviation-innovation hub in Karachi.
credit: Source: lh3.googleusercontent.com

name: National Bank of Pakistan Headquarters
desc: The head-office tower of Pakistan's largest state-owned bank on I.I. Chundrigar Road, the city's financial district.
credit: Wikimedia Commons

name: National Stadium Karachi
desc: Pakistan's premier cricket stadium, opened in 1955, host to international matches and a hub of the city's cricket culture.
credit: Wikimedia Commons

name: Native Jetty Bridge
desc: A historic bridge over the Chinna Creek linking Saddar to Kiamari, beside the Port Grand complex.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Pakistan Air Force Museum
desc: An aviation museum near Faisal Base displaying historic aircraft, radars and PAF memorabilia in indoor and outdoor exhibits.
credit: Source: Official Website

name: Port Muhammad Bin Qasim
desc: Pakistan's second major seaport, east of the city near the Indus delta, handling bulk cargo, containers and energy imports.
credit: Source: lh3.googleusercontent.com

name: PSO House
desc: The headquarters tower of Pakistan State Oil, a prominent office building in the Clifton area.
credit: Source: i.brecorder.com

name: Railway Station Cant
desc: In the latter half of the 19th century, PWD officials architected this station.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Richmond Crawford Veterinary Hospital (RCVH)
desc: A Victorian-era hospital dedicated to animal care.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Sindh Assembly
desc: One of the stone blocks that forms the steps immediately reveals its heritage.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Sindh Secretariat
desc: The administrative headquarters of the Government of Sindh, a colonial-era complex of government offices in Saddar.
credit: Source: i.tribune.com.pk

name: Spencer Eye Hospital
desc: Established in 1940, once one of the most reputed hospitals.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: State Bank of Pakistan
desc: The Bank of Calcutta was set up in the beginning of the 19th century, a precursor institution.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Teen Talwar Monument
desc: An iconic monument of three marble swords in Clifton, a well-known city landmark.
credit: Wikimedia Commons

name: The Ocean Mall
desc: A shopping mall and high-rise complex on the Clifton seafront near Dolmen Mall.
credit: Wikimedia Commons

name: Bai Virbaiji Parsi High School
desc: An evocative black and white photograph tells the school's history.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Bristol Hotel
desc: First time visitors are instantly struck by the palatial size of the edifice.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Chaukandi Tomb
desc: An early Islamic cemetery situated 29 km from Karachi.
credit: Wikimedia Commons

name: Commissioners Office
desc: Located at the office of the Deputy District Officer's Central Control Room.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Cotton Exchange
desc: Karachi of the early 20th century mostly had classically designed or revivalist buildings.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Denso Hall
desc: A library at the corner of M.A. Jinnah Road and Murad Khan Road.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Edward House
desc: It is a setting pretty much out of the famous Ayn Rand novel, The Fountainhead.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: EFU House
desc: Built in 1957 by Qamardin Jaffer Valliani and Qamardin Hashwani across from the KPT.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Federico Fellini
desc: Rainbow Centre in Saddar is no more than three decades old.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Ferer Hall
desc: Frere Hall is one of the many remnant buildings of the British Colonial era
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Ghulam Hoosain Khalikdina Hall and Library
desc: Local Christian and Hindu humanitarians, along with the British, contributed to its creation.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)
img: Ghulam Hoosain Khalikdina Hall and Library.jpg

name: Government College for Women
desc: This area was developed in the 1930s using reinforced concrete construction.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Hindu Gymkhana
desc: This amazing piece of construction has had its share of uncalled-for problems.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Indus Valley (Nusserwanjee) Building
desc: Nusserwanjee building which was relocated from old Kharadar area to IVS campus.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Jamshed Memorial Hall
desc: A colonial-era public hall named after Jamshed Nusserwanjee, Karachi's first elected mayor.
credit: Source: www.kbcuratorial.com

name: Jinnah Courts Hostel
desc: Built 1932-1933, protected under the Sindh Cultural Heritage Act.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Karachi Central Jail
desc: Once located at the northern edge of the city, completed in 1906.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Karachi Chamber of Commerce
desc: In 1860, Karachi began to burgeon into a commercially important town.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Karachi General Post Office (GPO)
desc: The city's central post office, a red-stone Indo-Gothic colonial building on I.I. Chundrigar Road.
credit: Source: i.tribune.com.pk

name: Karachi Goan Association Hall
desc: Located near the Karachi Grammar School.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Karachi Grammar School
desc: Once you are there, no need to think. Just imbibe the atmosphere. It is the KGS building.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Karachi War Cemetery
desc: A quiet Remembrance Ceremony is held here every second Sunday of November.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Katrak Mansion
desc: Named after the great philanthropist and Karachi lover.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Khyber Hotel
desc: One of the most out of the ordinary buildings on Preedy Street.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: KMC Building
desc: When you get closer to the entrance, the first thing that strikes you is its grandeur.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Kothari Prade
desc: Jehangir Kothari Parade is a promenade waterfront elevated sandstone structure.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Kumar Cinema
desc: Karachi has some really old cinema houses without clear construction dates.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Lakshmi Building
desc: Has a clock tower which sometimes loses badly in the race against time.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Lea Market
desc: For those who do not eat fish, the smell can be suffocating.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Mama Parsi Girls High School
desc: If this sounds like an over-the-board observation, come and visit.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Manora Light House
desc: The tallest lighthouse in the country, inaugurated April 1, 1889.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Mazar-e-Quaid
desc: Also known as the Jinnah Mausoleum or the National Mausoleum.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Merewether Clock Tower
desc: Merewether Clock Tower is located in Karachi, Sindh.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Mohatta Palace Museum
desc: For ancient mariners, the delta of the Indus served as an important geographical point.
credit: Wikimedia Commons)

name: Moriswala School
desc: Daud Kamal was an intelligent man and fine English poet connected to this school.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Mules Mansions
desc: If you do not buy the notion that buildings speak, try and talk to Mules Mansion.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Napier Barracks
desc: Flanks the road, now known as Liaquat Barracks.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: National Museum Of Pakistan
desc: The country's principal museum, in Burnes Garden, with galleries on the Indus Valley Civilization, Gandhara art and the Pakistan Movement.
credit: Wikimedia Commons

name: NJV School building
desc: Established in 1855 as a primary school, originally near Dow Medical College.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Overseas Investors Chamber of Commerce and Industry (OICCI)
desc: Has a stately and elegant look.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)
img: Overseas Investors Chamber of Commerce and Industry (OICCI).jpg

name: Pakistan Maritime Museum
desc: A naval-heritage museum near the coast displaying ships, aircraft and a submarine, run by the Pakistan Navy.
credit: Wikimedia Commons

name: Pakistan Stock Exchange Building
desc: The seat of Pakistan's stock exchange on I.I. Chundrigar Road, the country's principal securities market.
credit: Source: i.tribune.com.pk

name: PIDC House
desc: A landmark modernist office building near the Metropole area, headquarters of the Pakistan Industrial Development Corporation.
credit: Wikimedia Commons

name: Qasim Fort, Manora
desc: Also known as Manora Fort, established in the 18th century by the Talpur dynasty.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Quaid-e-Azam House and Museum
desc: Also known as Flagstaff House, a museum dedicated to Quaid-e-Azam.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Radio Pakistan
desc: Located opposite the N. J. V. School in an old Karachi neighbourhood.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Sindh High Court
desc: Originally called the Judicial Commissioners Court, construction began in 1923.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Sindh Madressahtul Islam College
desc: Entering the SMI from Gate 2, you chance upon a library.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Sindh Police Museum
desc: There is a dearth of quality museums in Karachi.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Sindh Wildlife Department Building
desc: Once known as one of the spookiest places in Karachi.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Soparivala Block
desc: Built by Seth Shapurji Hormusji Soparivala, who arrived in Karachi in the 1850s.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: St Joseph Convent School
desc: Situated in the busy Saddar region next to St Patricks Cathedral.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: The Chimney
desc: It is a chimney's tale. Perhaps a non story of a chimney where the smokestack plays no part.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Victoria and Elbert Museum (Supreme Court Building)
desc: The Registry was established on 14th October, 1957 in a borrowed wing of the High Court.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)
img: Victoria and Elbert Museum (Supreme Court Building).jpg

name: Wazir Mansion, Kharadar
desc: The parents of Muhammad Ali Jinnah once lived in the Wazir Mansion.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: YWCA Building
desc: Simplicity sometimes achieves feats that ornamentation does not even come close to.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Bahadurabad Market
desc: A busy commercial and shopping hub in the Bahadurabad neighbourhood.
credit: Wikimedia Commons

name: Boulton Market
desc: Built in the late 1800s, one of the oldest markets in Karachi.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Empress Market
desc: A famous marketplace situated in Saddar Town.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Jodia baazar
desc: One of Karachi's oldest and largest wholesale markets in the old city, trading in spices, groceries and general goods.
credit: Source: encrypted-tbn0.gstatic.com

name: Soldier Bazaar Market
desc: Made in 1954, the market is for sure something to look at.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Tariq Road Market
desc: A bustling shopping street in PECHS famous for clothing, footwear and accessories.
credit: Source: airial.travel

name: Zainab Market
desc: Built with great affection, like jilted lovers, victims of unrequited love.
credit: Courtesy Endowment Fund Trust for the Preservation of the Heritage of Sindh (heritage.eftsindh.com)

name: Boat Basin
desc: A long-running open-air food and dining strip in Clifton, known for street food, barbecue and juice stalls.
credit: Source: scontent.fkhi2-2.fna.fbcdn.net

name: Burns Road Food Street
desc: Karachi's most famous food street in the old city, home to decades-old nihari, biryani and dessert establishments.
credit: Source: www.arabnews.com

name: DHA Phase 2 Food Street
desc: A modern open-air dining strip in the Defence Housing Authority area.
credit: Source: lh3.googleusercontent.com

name: Hussainabad Food Street
desc: A local food street in the Federal B Area known for barbecue and street snacks.
credit: Source: thrustreets.com

name: Kharadar food street
